mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both consumer prices (CPI) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of cases where both CPIAUCSL and HOUST decline, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.5 signals robust supply-side growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.5, or the signal's IC falls below 0.10 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day moving average crossover of CPI inflation above 2.5% and FEDFUNDS below 1.5% signals stagflation risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 70%+ of cases where both CPIAUCSL > 2.5 and FEDFUNDS < 1.5, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real sales (RSAFS) below 670k combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 3.7%) signals deteriorating consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of cases where RSAFS < 670k and UNRATE > 3.7, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month consecutive decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals a tight labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 3.5, or the signal's IC falls below 0.12 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.5 combined with rising housing starts (HOUST > 1700) signals strong supply-side momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 70%+ of cases where both INDPRO > 105.5 and HOUST > 1700, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
